primary_sources.title: The Update Framework Specification 1.0.34
primary_sources.relevant_claim: Clients must resist rollback, indefinite freeze, and mix-and-match attacks; timestamp metadata exists to prevent replay of outdated signed metadata.

primary_sources.title: SLSA Provenance v1.2
primary_sources.relevant_claim: Provenance binds artifacts and build invocation details but does not by itself provide a monotonic promotion history for a relying system.

finding: Same-digest trust composition is insufficient to prevent reuse of an older, once-valid promotion decision. A relying system needs trusted monotonic state that rejects lower sequence numbers, sequence gaps, and same-sequence different-subject equivocation while permitting idempotent same-subject replay.
design_implication: Place a monotonic sequence gate after cryptographic and policy verification and before durable promotion. Persist the returned next_trusted_state atomically with the promoted artifact reference.

verification.method: isolated Node test run against byte-identical pre-commit files

uncertainty: The gate assumes trusted state integrity and atomic persistence supplied by the caller.
The gate does not establish globally unique ordering across multiple independent writers.
The gate performs no signature, certificate, transparency, timestamp, or digest verification.
falsification_route: Submit a lower sequence, skip a sequence, or reuse a known sequence with a different subject digest and observe fail-closed rejection. Corrupt or roll back the caller-managed trusted state to demonstrate the external state-integrity dependency.
claim_boundary: Acceptance means only that the candidate is monotonic relative to caller-supplied trusted state; it does not prove the underlying evidence claim true.
